# Copilot Instructions for UserAdminModule

## Project Overview

UserAdminModule is a PSGallery-published PowerShell function management framework. It gives administrators a dynamic, folder-based system for organising and importing their own PowerShell functions — without dot-sourcing loose scripts or maintaining individual manifests.

**Repository:** `https://github.com/BanterBoy/UserAdminModule`  
**Published:** PowerShell Gallery — `Install-Module UserAdminModule -AllowPrerelease`  
**Targets:** PowerShell 5.1 and 7+, dual-compatible unless explicitly noted

### Structure at a Glance

```
UserAdminModule/
├── UserAdminModule.psd1          ← PSGallery manifest (v1.0.0-preview1)
├── UserAdminModule.psm1          ← Root module — loads Private/, Public/, Shell/
├── Public/                       ← 5 exported framework functions
│   ├── Import-PersonalModules.ps1
│   ├── Initialize-UserAdminModule.ps1
│   ├── Invoke-FunctionIndexRegeneration.ps1
│   ├── Invoke-PersonalModulesMenu.ps1
│   └── New-PSM1Module.ps1
├── Private/                      ← Internal helpers (not exported)
│   └── Get-UserAdminModuleConfig.ps1
├── Shell/                        ← Bundled UX submodule (loaded with -Global)
│   ├── Shell.psm1
│   └── Public/                   ← 16 functions: Set-PromptisAdmin, New-Greeting, etc.
├── profiles/                     ← Reference profile scripts for users
├── resources/                    ← Data files and generator scripts
│   ├── New-ModuleMenuApp.ps1     ← Generates ModuleMenuApp.html from FunctionIndex.json
│   └── ModuleMenuApp.html        ← Regenerated — not manually edited
├── FunctionIndex.json            ← Auto-generated by Invoke-FunctionIndexRegeneration
├── FunctionIndex.md              ← Auto-generated by Invoke-FunctionIndexRegeneration
├── build/                        ← Publish-ToGallery.ps1 (excluded from package)
└── .github/                      ← CI/CD workflows and AI guidance (excluded from package)
```

### How the Module Works

1. User runs `Initialize-UserAdminModule -Path 'C:\MyModules'` — writes `CustomModulesPath` to `$env:APPDATA\UserAdminModule\config.json`
2. On `Import-Module UserAdminModule`, Shell submodule loads globally — prompt helpers available immediately
3. `Import-PersonalModules -Category <Tab>` discovers categories dynamically by scanning for `<FolderName>.psm1` files in both the module root and `CustomModulesPath`
4. `Get-UserAdminModuleConfig` (Private/) reads config.json — never call this from outside the module
5. `Invoke-FunctionIndexRegeneration` auto-discovers both paths from config and writes `FunctionIndex.json` to the module root

---

## CRITICAL Conventions — Zero Tolerance

### Error Handling

**USE `trap` STATEMENTS ONLY. NEVER try/catch.**

```powershell
trap {
    Write-Error "Failed to do X: $_"
    break
}
```

- `break` for fatal/unrecoverable errors
- `continue` inside loops where per-item failures are acceptable
- Shell.psm1 uses try/catch — this is legacy, do not replicate

### Advanced Functions

ALWAYS use `[CmdletBinding()]`. Every function must support `-Verbose` and `-Debug`.

```powershell
function Verb-Noun {
    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(...)
}
```

### Approved Verbs

ONLY use approved PowerShell verbs. Verify with `Get-Verb`. Common approved verbs:
`Get-`, `Set-`, `New-`, `Remove-`, `Invoke-`, `Start-`, `Stop-`, `Export-`, `Import-`,
`Enable-`, `Disable-`, `Test-`, `Find-`, `Add-`, `Update-`, `Initialize-`, `Open-`, `Install-`

### String Interpolation — CRITICAL

Variables followed by `:` or `.` in strings MUST use `$()` subexpression syntax.

```powershell
# CORRECT
"Failed for $($Category): $_"
"Processing $($module.Name): done"

# WRONG — causes parser errors
"Failed for $Category: $_"
"Processing $module.Name: done"
```

### Parameter Validation

ALWAYS validate parameters:

```powershell
[Parameter(Mandatory)]
[ValidateNotNullOrEmpty()]
[string]$Path

[ValidateSet('Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday','Saturday','Sunday')]
[string]$Day
```

### Comment-Based Help

ALWAYS include for every function:

```powershell
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    One-line description.
.DESCRIPTION
    Full description.
.PARAMETER Name
    What this parameter does.
.EXAMPLE
    Verb-Noun -Parameter Value
    What this example demonstrates.
.NOTES
    Author: Luke Leigh
    Tested on: PowerShell 5.1 and 7+
#>
```

### Output

NEVER use `Write-Host`. Use:
- `Write-Verbose` — diagnostic detail (shown with `-Verbose`)
- `Write-Warning` — non-fatal issues the user should know about
- `Write-Error` — failures (usually in `trap`)
- `Write-Information` — informational output (`-InformationAction Continue`)
- Return values via the pipeline — no `return` needed for pipeline output

### PS 5.1 / 7+ Compatibility

- **No `#Requires -PSEdition Core`** in any Shell/Public file — Shell.psm1 dot-sources the whole folder; a Core-only `#Requires` crashes the entire load in PS 5.1
- **No `IValidateSetValuesGenerator`** — PS 6.0+ only interface; use hardcoded `[ValidateSet(...)]` instead
- **No `??=` operator** — PS 7+ only; use `if (-not $x) { $x = ... }` instead
- **No `Join-String`** — availability varies; use `-join` operator instead
- Test in both editions when possible

---

## Module-Specific Patterns

### $script:UAMModuleRoot

Set at the top of `UserAdminModule.psm1` to `$PSScriptRoot`. All Public functions that do
submodule discovery MUST reference `$Script:UAMModuleRoot` instead of `$PSScriptRoot` —
because after moving to `Public/`, `$PSScriptRoot` resolves to `Public/`, not the module root.

```powershell
$_uamRoot = if ($Script:UAMModuleRoot) { $Script:UAMModuleRoot } else { Split-Path $PSScriptRoot -Parent }
```

### Config Pattern

Config is stored at `$env:APPDATA\UserAdminModule\config.json`. Always use `Get-UserAdminModuleConfig`
(Private/) to read it — never read the JSON directly from Public functions.

### Submodule Discovery

A folder qualifies as a discoverable submodule if it contains `<FolderName>.psm1`.
`Public/` and `Private/` directories are NOT picked up as false positives (no matching `.psm1`).

### FunctionIndex

`FunctionIndex.json` and `FunctionIndex.md` are auto-generated by `Invoke-FunctionIndexRegeneration`.
**Never edit them manually.** They live at the module root and are included in the PSGallery package
so that `Open-ModuleMenuApp` works immediately after install.

---

## Where New Code Belongs

| New Code Type | Target Location |
|---|---|
| Framework function (user-facing) | `Public/` — must be added to `FunctionsToExport` in `.psd1` |
| Internal helper | `Private/` — never exported |
| Shell UX function | `Shell/Public/` — loaded with `-Global` by the psm1 |
| Reference profile | `profiles/` |
| Data file / template | `resources/` |
| Build / publish tooling | `build/` |
| CI workflows | `.github/workflows/` |

---

## PSGallery Publishing Rules

- `build/` and `.github/` are excluded from the package (see `Publish-ToGallery.ps1`)
- `FunctionIndex.json` and `FunctionIndex.md` ARE included — regenerate before publishing
- `ModuleMenuApp.html` IS included — regenerate with `Open-ModuleMenuApp -Regenerate` before publishing
- Bump `ModuleVersion` in `UserAdminModule.psd1` before every publish
- Pre-release: set `Prerelease = 'preview2'` etc. in `PrivateData.PSData`
- Stable release: remove the `Prerelease` line entirely

---

## Available Skills

Load the relevant SKILL.md file BEFORE generating any code for domain-specific tasks:

| Skill | File | Use When |
|---|---|---|
| `useradminmodule` | `.github/skills/useradminmodule/SKILL.md` | Creating/finding/refactoring any function in this module |
| `new-function-tests` | `.github/skills/new-function-tests/SKILL.md` | Creating any new function — tests are MANDATORY |
| `pester-testing` | `.github/skills/pester-testing/SKILL.md` | Writing tests for existing functions or CI setup |
| `documentation-generation` | `.github/skills/documentation-generation/SKILL.md` | Updating README, generating function catalogs |
| `github-management` | `.github/skills/github-management/SKILL.md` | Releases, issue templates, PRs, changelogs |

---

## Quality Checklist — Before Any Commit

- [ ] All functions use `[CmdletBinding()]`
- [ ] All error handling uses `trap` — zero `try/catch` blocks
- [ ] All variables before `:` or `.` in strings use `$()`
- [ ] No `Write-Host` — only `Write-Verbose`, `Write-Warning`, `Write-Error`, `Write-Information`
- [ ] Comment-based help present (SYNOPSIS, DESCRIPTION, PARAMETER, EXAMPLE, NOTES)
- [ ] No `#Requires -PSEdition Core` in Shell/Public files
- [ ] New Public functions added to `FunctionsToExport` in `.psd1`
- [ ] Test file created alongside any new function (see `new-function-tests` skill)
- [ ] `FunctionIndex.json` regenerated after adding/removing functions
- [ ] `ModuleVersion` bumped in `.psd1` if publishing
